symptoms.symptom_name: Gross angular deformity with an audible crack
Bleeding wound overlying the fracture site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defines an open (compound) fracture with infection risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to bear weight or move the ankle
Currently normal toe colour, warmth, and sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring currently, but requires ongoing monitoring as this can change.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bony crepitus and visible protruding bone fragment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Deformity, an overlying wound, and functional loss together confirm an open displaced fracture requiring emergency transport.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency transport with professional splinting, X-ray imaging, IV antibiotics, and surgical washout/fixation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard management for an open, displaced fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ongoing neurovascular observation (colour, warmth, sensation, pulses) en route and in hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects any evolving compartment syndrome or vascular compromise.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting to splint or straighten the leg at home before the ambulance arrives
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Risks further neurovascular and soft tissue injury; should be left to trained paramedics.
